summary.intent: Fix a Codex review finding on PR #680 where the slider component's thumb element unconditionally emitted aria-labelledby referencing a label ID that may not exist in the DOM.
summary.outcome: Added a SetHasLabel tracking event/context flag to the slider core so aria-labelledby is only emitted when the label part has actually been rendered; updated spec, added a regression test, accepted updated snapshots, and committed the fix with Codex co-authorship.

repo: The project uses a waiting-for-codex-review skill workflow: post @codex review comment, poll for 👀 reaction (in-progress) and 👍 reaction (approval), then address any unresolved Codex threads before re-triggering.
cargo xci-fast runs a 10-gate CI pipeline (fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter gates) and must pass before pushing review fixes.

cargo insta unreferenced-snapshot checks cannot be scoped with --glob-filter or package filters without marking unrelated component snapshots as unreferenced; the full `cargo insta test --package ars-components --features i18n --unreferenced=reject` must be used.
Snapshot fixture updates for aria attribute changes are accepted via `cargo insta test --package ars-components --accept` rather than `cargo insta accept` alone.
The slider spec at spec/components/input/slider.md is the authoritative contract and must be updated in sync with behavioral changes to the Rust implementation.

code.finding: Thumb attribute rendering previously always emitted aria-labelledby to a label ID even when no label part was rendered, creating a dangling ARIA reference. Fixed by gating on a new SetHasLabel context flag.
SetHasLabel event/context tracking was added here to allow adapters to report whether the label part has been rendered before thumb attributes are computed.
Spec documents the aria-labelledby conditional behavior and the SetHasLabel event contract; both were updated to reflect the gated emission logic.

workflow: TDD red-green cycle was followed: write failing test first, confirm it fails for the right reason, then implement the fix to make it pass.
cargo llvm-cov was used for coverage validation scoped to the affected module after the fix.
Post-review commits should include Co-authored-by: Codex trailer when the fix directly addresses a Codex review finding.
Codex review polling uses GitHub GraphQL API to check reviewThread resolution state and GitHub REST API for emoji reactions as the approval signal.

friction: cargo insta's --glob-filter and package-scoped filters incorrectly flagged unrelated snapshots as unreferenced, requiring multiple failed attempts before falling back to full-package invocation.
cargo insta accept and cargo insta test --accept behaved differently; had to try multiple invocation forms to accept the changed slider snapshots.
xci-fast is slow (10 gates) and had to be run twice: once before the initial PR and again after the review fix, adding significant wall-clock time.
Codex review poll loop required 120-second sleep intervals with no way to accelerate, leading to extended waiting periods between status updates.
open_items: Other components (number_input, switch) may have similar unconditional aria-labelledby patterns that were identified during research but not fixed in this PR.

The insta unreferenced-snapshot check scoping limitation should be investigated — a workspace-level solution or filter form that doesn't false-positive on unrelated components would reduce friction in future snapshot updates.
Codex review second pass not yet triggered at transcript end — PR still needs re-review after the fix commit is pushed.
